title: notifications core — retention windows
description: Retention per entity. Hot = searchable + indexed. Cold = archived (present but not indexed). Purge = hard-delete. Every purge emits an audit row in the audit_log tier so the trail survives the payload. Retention_days per plan comes from the entitlement.
compliance_regimes_referenced: casl s.6 (consent trail — while service in use + 3 years)

cold.description: Archived (soft-delete + search-index removed). Still queryable by delivery_id for support.

purge.trigger: cold window exceeded OR user erasure request
purge.action: hard delete; write anonymised audit row (retains occurred_at + category + delivery outcome, no PII)

hot.description: Hot for delivery-diagnostics dashboard + provider-failure retry window.

cold.description: Cold for compliance evidence (which category, when, delivered). No PII in the delivery row itself — recipient is on the parent Notification.

hot.description: Templates are hot as long as they're used.

cold.description: 7 years cold retention post-archive for SOC 2 CC8.1 change management evidence. Every template version is retained — audit rows point at specific versions.

purge.trigger: no live sends reference the row AND cold window exceeded

cold.description: No cold tier. Preferences are current state, not historical record.
No retention. is_system=true categories cannot be deleted; tenant-override rows can be deleted by tenant admin if no live Notification references them within the last 90d.

hot.description: Digest state kept 30 days after delivery for audit + support (why was this specific notification in that specific digest).
Hot for compliance queries + DSAR aggregation.

cold.description: 7 year cold retention. Standard for SOC 2 evidence + safeguarding-adjacent audit trails.

compliance_tier_inheritance.note: Every field on every entity is classified in foundation's 8-tier taxonomy via data-classes.json. Retention here overlays that classification.
